stories.headline: Driver arrested after running red light, striking 3 pedestrians in downtown Denver crosswalk
stories.summary:
A driver ran a red light in downtown Denver on June 14 and struck three pedestrians who were crossing within a marked crosswalk, according to a probable cause statement from Denver Police. Two of the victims were thrown into the air by the force of the impact, while a third was pulled beneath the vehicle and dragged nearly half a block before the car was stopped in traffic.

The suspect, identified as Cesario Serrato, 56, was arrested in connection with the crash. Police initially described the incident as a right-turn collision before clarifying that Serrato had run a red light prior to hitting the victims.

All three pedestrians sustained serious injuries. The case has drawn widespread attention as an example of the dangers pedestrians face at downtown intersections. Investigators are continuing to review the circumstances of the crash.

stories.headline: Colorado receives FDA approval to import lower-cost prescription drugs from Canada
stories.summary:
Colorado has become only the second state in the country to receive federal approval to import prescription drugs from Canada, Gov. Jared Polis announced Monday. The FDA signed off on the state's application after a process that began with legislation Polis signed in 2019 and included a formal plan submitted to the FDA in 2022 with multiple rounds of revisions.

Colorado's approved plan covers 20 specific medications from 10 manufacturers, including widely used drugs such as Ozempic and Eliquis. State officials estimate savings of 20% to 60% per prescription, amounting to roughly $46 million in reduced insurance premiums and out-of-pocket costs over three years.

Polis said the next steps involve securing a distributor and establishing a supply chain, a process he hopes can be completed within a year. Colorado is the second state after Florida to receive such approval, though Florida has yet to import any drugs. Every batch imported will be independently tested before reaching patients, a requirement built into the FDA's approval.

stories.headline: Denver City Council approves new lobbying disclosure rules requiring detailed reporting every two months
stories.summary:
The Denver City Council gave final approval Monday to an ordinance expanding disclosure requirements for lobbyists who seek to influence city government. Under the new rules, anyone paid at least $1,000 to lobby must report every two months which officials they met with, how much they were paid, what proposals they supported or opposed, and who their clients are — a significant expansion from the prior requirement to disclose only their names and clients.

The measure, developed by Denver Clerk and Recorder Paul López's office in partnership with three council members, also extends the definition of lobbying to include grassroots organizations that direct residents to contact council members about pending votes.

A new cooling-off period provision bars former elected officials and mayoral appointees from lobbying the city for at least six months after leaving office. The rules are intended to prevent officials from leveraging relationships formed in office for future employment. The ordinance takes effect in January.

stories.headline: Colorado lawmakers move to limit PERA bonuses after Sun investigation revealed millions paid during losing years
stories.summary:
A Democratic state senator says he plans to introduce legislation next year to cap bonuses paid to Colorado Public Employees' Retirement Association investment staff, following a Colorado Sun investigation that found PERA paid out $10.2 million in performance bonuses in the year after its portfolio lost $9.8 billion in 2022. Nine investment officials received more than $400,000 each in that payout cycle, despite the overall losses.

Sen. Chris Kolker, a Democrat from Centennial and a personal financial adviser, said he intends to introduce a bill preventing PERA from paying bonuses when investments deliver negative returns, even if employees outperform their benchmark indexes. PERA has defended the practice by arguing it must compete with private-sector firms for investment talent.

The transparency legislation that enabled the Sun's investigation — Senate Bill 147, passed in 2025 — was co-sponsored by Republican Sen. Byron Pelton of Sterling, who stopped short of calling for specific bonus restrictions but said the optics were damaging to PERA's reputation. PERA declined to comment on the proposed legislation, saying it does not take positions on bills until they are formally introduced.

stories.headline: Denver chef Penelope Wong of Yuan Wonton wins James Beard Award for best chef in mountain U.S.
stories.summary:
Penelope Wong, the Denver native behind dumpling shop Yuan Wonton, was named the best chef in the mountain United States at the James Beard Awards ceremony Monday night in Chicago, earning the honor after three consecutive years of nominations. Wong opened Yuan Wonton in 2019 with her husband Rob Jenks and sous chef Ngoc Nguyen, starting as a food truck before the restaurant earned its first James Beard semifinalist recognition in 2023.

Accepting the award at the Lyric Opera of Chicago, Wong acknowledged fellow Denver nominee Johnny Curiel of Alma Fonda Fina and said the recognition was beyond her original ambitions for the restaurant. "To be honest, this was never the dream," she said from the podium.

Several other Colorado chefs and beverage professionals were nominated alongside Wong, including Josh Niernberg of Bin 707 Foodbar in Grand Junction for outstanding chef and Ryan Fletter of Barolo Grill in Denver for outstanding professional in beverage service. Yuan Wonton is located at 2878 Fairfax St. in Denver.

stories.headline: Nuggets defeat Thunder 119-107 in Game 6 to advance in Western Conference Semifinals
stories.summary:
The Denver Nuggets defeated the Oklahoma City Thunder 119-107 in Game 6 of the Western Conference Semifinals, advancing in the playoffs despite playing without four starters and losing their most important backup during the series. The victory extends Denver's postseason run under challenging injury circumstances.

The Nuggets' resilience through the injury-depleted roster drew attention heading into the next round. Details on individual player performances and the opponent Denver will face next were not immediately available from the sourced reporting.

The win also comes amid ongoing contract extension discussions for star center Nikola Jokic, who is approaching eligibility for what is expected to be a historic extension. Front office decisions on Jokic and the broader roster are expected to dominate the offseason conversation.

stories.headline: Colorado Rockies set franchise record with 23-9 victory over Athletics in 101-degree heat
stories.summary:
The Colorado Rockies set a franchise record for runs scored Sunday, defeating the Oakland Athletics 23-9 in a game played at Las Vegas Ballpark in 101-degree heat. Willi Castro drove in seven runs and Hunter Goodman recorded five hits in the record-setting offensive performance.

The win was part of a stretch in which Colorado's younger players have propelled the team to its third series victory in its last four. The Rockies debuted pitching prospect Sean Sullivan in a separate game against the Athletics as part of the team's ongoing evaluation of its young roster.

The performance stands as a notable positive milestone for a Rockies franchise that has struggled in recent seasons. The record run total marks a benchmark for the organization regardless of its overall standing in the National League West.

stories.headline: Update: Broncos linebacker Jonathon Cooper arrested second time in a week; forensic exam reveals strangulation injury to girlfriend
stories.summary:
Denver Broncos linebacker Jonathon Cooper was arrested for the second time in a week, with a forensic examination revealing that his girlfriend sustained a strangulation injury, according to reports. The escalating legal situation substantially increases the likelihood of NFL discipline, with analysts suggesting a six-game suspension is a real possibility.

Cooper's repeated arrests within a short timeframe have put his roster status in question as the Broncos are in the midst of OTAs. The team has not yet made a public statement about Cooper's future with the organization.

The NFL has a personal conduct policy that can result in suspension independent of criminal proceedings. Given the nature of the allegations and the forensic findings, league officials are widely expected to open their own investigation.

stories.headline: Red Robin plans to sell 86 more restaurant locations to pay down debt
stories.summary:
Red Robin, the Arapahoe County-based burger chain, plans to sell 86 additional restaurant locations as part of an effort to reduce its debt load following deals struck earlier this spring. The Greenwood Village-headquartered company has been restructuring its business model amid continued financial pressures in the casual dining sector.

The planned divestitures are part of a broader strategy by Red Robin management to stabilize the company's balance sheet. The chain operates hundreds of locations across the United States.

The move reflects ongoing challenges facing mid-tier casual dining brands competing against fast-casual alternatives. Red Robin has been working to reposition itself in the market while managing debt obligations that accumulated in recent years.

stories.headline: JBS USA to close Pennsylvania and Tennessee beef plants, lay off nearly 1,700 workers amid cattle shortage
stories.summary:
JBS USA, the Brazilian-owned meat processing giant headquartered in Greeley, Colorado, announced it is closing a beef production plant in Pennsylvania and a production facility in Memphis, Tennessee, resulting in layoffs affecting 1,693 workers. The closures are driven by a U.S. cattle shortage that has pressured meat processors' supply chains and profit margins.

JBS is one of the largest employers in Colorado's agricultural sector and a significant presence in the state's economy. The domestic cattle herd has been at historic lows in recent years, forcing processors to reduce capacity to align with available supply.

The closures add to concerns about the long-term health of the domestic beef processing industry as ranchers have been slow to rebuild herds following years of drought and high feed costs. JBS has not announced any changes to its Colorado operations at this time.

stories.headline: Water World in Federal Heights set to open largest new development since 1986 with Summit Canyon area
stories.summary:
Water World, the 70-acre water park in Federal Heights north of Denver, is on track to open its new Summit Canyon area this month, marking the facility's largest expansion since 1986. The approximately 2.5-acre addition will be anchored by a 60-foot Lookout Tower — the park's new highest point — providing access to four new waterslides including two tube rides and two body flumes.

The development will introduce the Denver metro area's first drop-capsule slide, in which riders enter an enclosed capsule and are released through a trapdoor floor. Summit Canyon will also include a 700-person seating area, a pool with an obstacle course, and a pool with an in-water basketball court.

The new section will occupy the footprint of the former Calypso Cove area, which has since closed. An official opening date has not been announced on the Water World website, with the park advising guests to watch for an update this season.

stories.headline: Metro Denver inflation hits 5% in May, highest rate since 2023, driven by energy costs
stories.summary:
Inflation in the Denver metro area reached 5% in May, the highest rate recorded locally since 2023, with rising energy costs identified as the primary driver. The figure outpaces the national inflation rate and signals renewed price pressure for Denver-area households after a period of relative moderation.

Energy prices have surged in recent months, affecting utility bills and transportation costs across the region. Analysts note that elevated energy costs can ripple through the broader economy by raising the cost of goods, services, and construction.

The increase comes as the Denver metro housing market is also under pressure from high interest rates and declining home sales. Together, the trends suggest that affordability challenges for Denver-area residents are intensifying heading into the summer months.

weather.notable: About 10 degrees above the average high for this time of year!

joke.joke: My favorite machine at the gym is the vending machine.

topStory.headline: U.S. and Iran Sign Preliminary Peace Agreement, Key Details Unresolved as Formal Signing Set for Friday
topStory.summary:
The United States and Iran have reached a preliminary memorandum of understanding to end hostilities, with President Donald Trump announcing the agreement had been electronically signed by himself, Vice President JD Vance, and Iranian parliamentary speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf. A formal signing ceremony is scheduled for Friday in Geneva, Switzerland, where the next round of negotiations on Iran's nuclear program, U.S. sanctions relief, and the possible release of frozen Iranian assets will also begin. Trump told the New York Times he would restart military operations if Iran failed to reach a nuclear agreement during broader talks set to follow.

The document, described by Vance as roughly one and a half pages and characterized as "very general," leaves numerous critical issues to be resolved in future technical negotiations. Vance confirmed on CNN that no sanctions relief or unfrozen assets have been transferred to Iran, stating that such benefits are contingent on Iran meeting its obligations under the agreement. He also told NBC News that International Atomic Energy Agency inspectors would be allowed back into Iran, and that a core provision of the deal requires Iran and the U.S. to cooperate in destroying Iran's stockpile of highly enriched uranium.

Shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, a chokepoint for roughly 20 percent of the world's oil supply, remained largely stalled as of Monday evening despite the announced deal. Vessel-tracking data showed only four tankers and several smaller craft had successfully transited the strait since the agreement was announced, with the majority of tankers remaining anchored in the Gulf of Oman and the Persian Gulf. Senior U.S. officials briefed that the strait would formally reopen on Friday, coinciding with the Geneva signing ceremony.

Iran's Foreign Minister Seyed Abbas Araghchi confirmed that new negotiations toward a final peace agreement would begin in Switzerland on Friday. Trump is currently attending the G7 summit in France, where a special session on Iran is scheduled Tuesday with the leaders of Egypt, Qatar, and the United Arab Emirates. G7 allies including the United Kingdom and France had been leading plans for a joint mission to protect vessels in the strait, though Trump indicated he did not believe significant outside assistance would be necessary.

The preliminary agreement has drawn criticism from former President Barack Obama, who said it was unlikely to differ substantially from the 2015 Joint Comprehensive Plan of Action, the nuclear deal Trump withdrew from during his first term. Critics and analysts noted the lack of published text and the number of unresolved questions, including the precise terms governing Iran's nuclear activities, the status of Lebanon, and the timeline for any sanctions relief. Vance indicated Trump may release the MOU publicly before the Friday ceremony rather than waiting until after it is formally signed.
